title: Entanglement-assisted continuous-variable concatenated codes for encoding qubits or oscillators
authors: Nihar Ranjan Dash, Sanjoy Dutta, R. Srikanth, Subhashish Banerjee
abstract: Entanglement-assisted (EA) stabilizer codes enhance the rate of error correction in relation to codes with no pre-shared entanglement. Meanwhile, bosonic error-correcting codes, such as the Gottesman-Kitaev-Preskill (GKP) code, can be concatenated with qubit stabilizer codes to significantly reduce the logical failure probability of those stabilizer codes. First, we combine the above two concepts to propose an EA version of the qubit-into-oscillators concatenated code that chains an EA-stabilizer (outer) code with a GKP (inner) code. As an example we present a three-qubit EA-repetition concatenated with a GKP code. Second, we propose an EA version of the non-Gaussian oscillator-into-oscillators concatenated code that chains a GKP (outer) code with an EA-stabilizer (inner) code. As an example we present a GKP code concatenated with a three-qubit EA repetition code that uses two maximally entangled modes (emodes) and suppresses the variances of both position and momentum quadrature errors of a data mode. Furthermore, we generalize the latter example to a family of GKP code concatenated with a $n$-qubit EA repetition code that uses ${n-1}$ emodes and suppresses the variances of both position and momentum quadrature errors of a data mode by a factor ${1/n}$.
summary: This paper develops novel entanglement-assisted continuous-variable concatenated codes by combining EA stabilizer codes with GKP bosonic codes. It theoretically demonstrates that these concatenated structures effectively suppress quadrature errors, achieving a variance reduction proportional to $1/n$. This advances the field of fault-tolerant quantum computation for bosonic systems.
main_problem: The research aims to enhance quantum error correction rates by combining entanglement-assisted (EA) stabilizer codes with bosonic continuous-variable (CV) codes, specifically the Gottesman-Kitaev-Preskill (GKP) code.
main_result: They propose and analyze two concatenated structures ($	ext{EA} 	riangleright 	ext{GKP}$ and $	ext{GKP} 	riangleright 	ext{EA}$) that demonstrate significant suppression of quadrature errors, achieving a variance reduction factor of $1/n$ in the general case.
method: The analysis involves deriving logical state representations for concatenated codes, calculating syndrome measurements from Gaussian unitary encoders, and employing Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) to estimate and correct residual quadrature errors.
model_or_system: The system models quantum information encoded in bosonic modes using GKP states. The theoretical framework utilizes stabilizer formalism generalized to include entanglement assistance via ebits for error correction.
key_observables: Logical failure probability ($p_L$), residual quadrature error variances ($	ext{Var}(\zeta^*_q) = 	ext{Var}(\zeta^*_p)$), and the success probability of bit identification ($p_{	ext{succ}}$).
important_parameters: The number of qubits/modes ($n$), the noise variance ($\sigma^2$), and the entanglement resource (ebits).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes independent and identically distributed (iid) Gaussian random shift errors, and for the general result, it relies on syndrome measurements falling within a specific range $[-\sqrt{\pi}/2, \sqrt{\pi}/2]$.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates encoding circuits for GKP Bell pairs using both computational and canonical bases. Figure 5 compares the logical failure probability versus standard deviation for different concatenation orders.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes the theoretical basis by combining EA concepts with bosonic codes (GKP). It then details two specific concatenation orders ($	ext{EA} 	riangleright 	ext{GKP}$ and $	ext{GKP} 	riangleright 	ext{EA}$) using examples like $n=3$. The core analysis involves deriving error suppression factors, culminating in a general theorem showing $1/n$ variance reduction for the $	ext{CGKP} 	riangleright C[[n,1,n;n-1]]$ code.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant as it pushes the boundaries of quantum information theory by merging two advanced QEC paradigms (EA and CV bosonic codes). The focus on error suppression factors provides concrete theoretical benchmarks for future experimental implementations in quantum optics or superconducting circuits.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 3: Encoding circuit for [[3, 1, 3; 2]]2 entanglement- assisted repetition code.

FIG. 2: (Color online) code structure of an C[[3,1,3;2]] ▷CGKP code with outer [[3, 1, 3; 2]] EA-repetition code and inner GKP code: A single logical qubit of the outer [[3, 1, 3; 2]] EA-repetition code represented as a white circle encoded into three physical qubits (blue circles) by utilizing two pre-shared entangled bits( green circles). The inner GKP code further encodes these three qubits, along with the two entangled bits, into harmonic oscillators, represented by yellow and red cir- cles, respectively.

FIG. 5: (Color online) Logical failure probability of C[[3,1,3;2]] ▷CGKP, C[[5,1,3]] ▷CGKP, and CGKP ▷C[[3,1,3;2]] codes as a function of the standard deviation σ, depicted as the blue, green, and blue plots, respectively.
